research_question: Does a policy network using contrastive-learned 8-dim representation outperform vanilla REINFORCE on raw 15-dim features?
key_finding: Frozen encoder (+0.36 chips/round vs baseline) confirms that the contrastive representation aids policy learning when used as a fixed feature extractor. Finetuning hurts, likely due to noisy REINFORCE gradients corrupting the encoder.
recommended_next_step: Two-phase training: Phase 1 contrastive pretraining (done), Phase 2 REINFORCE on frozen encoder with larger policy head or higher lr.
